stories: Iran conflict: ceasefire optics remain muddy, retaliation risk stays the dominant cross-asset catalyst
Oil is still the macro choke-point: OPEC+ talks more supply, but Hormuz constraints keep the risk premium sticky
Rates repricing is back in charge: stronger March employment pushes yields up and trims near-term cut expectations
US equities: S&P 500 snaps a 5-week losing streak, but the “war-path” remains the only story that matters
Russia–Ukraine: continued large-scale strikes hit energy/rail infrastructure, keeping Europe’s risk premium elevated
Credit stress watch: Reuters flags emerging strain in private lending (system-relevant, opaque, and prone to surprises)
Crypto risk-off: Bitcoin breaks down (reported move under ~$92K) as correlation spikes during geopolitical stress
Insider tape (M): Macy’s CEO Antony Spring sold shares at ~$18.08—
Options edge (AAL): whale call flow flagged in American Airlines—May 15, 2026 $8 calls, 100 contracts, split across 6 trades (~$25K premium)
Stablecoin regulation catalyst: Treasury rolls out GENIUS Act implementation via NPRM—tightening reserve/redemption expectations (and creating winners/losers)
